mantras.title: Maths: The Power of Zero
mantras.text: Zero is the ultimate stop sign of math: anything multiplied by 0 instantly becomes 0. But watch this twist—any number (except 0) raised to the power of 0 magically becomes 1!

mantras.title: Science: Space Silence
mantras.text: Even the biggest explosion in space would be completely silent. Sound needs air to travel, and space is almost empty—so it’s the quietest place in the universe.

mantras.title: Maths: The Lone Even Prime
mantras.text: The number 2 is a math superstar: it’s the only even prime number. Every other prime is odd, making 2 the loneliest and most special prime in the whole number line.

mantras.title: Science: Why the Sky is Blue
mantras.text: The sky is blue because tiny air molecules scatter blue sunlight more than other colors. Without our atmosphere, the daytime sky would look black—even on Earth!

mantras.title: Maths: Palindrome Numbers
mantras.text: A palindrome number reads the same backward as forward. 121, 1331, and 1001 are all ‘mirror’ numbers that love symmetry and playing tricks on the eye.

mantras.title: Science: Water’s Triple Identity
mantras.text: Water is the only common substance on Earth that naturally exists as solid ice, liquid water, and invisible steam—all at once, depending on the temperature.

mantras.title: Maths: The 10x Shortcut
mantras.text: To multiply any whole number by 10, just slap a 0 at the end! It’s the easiest math trick in the world, and it works every single time.

mantras.title: Science: Octopus with Three Hearts
mantras.text: An octopus has three hearts: two pump blood to the gills, and one moves blood around the rest of its squishy body. No wonder it can squeeze through tiny spaces!

mantras.title: Maths: Shapes with Edge-count Names
mantras.text: A hexagon has 6 sides, an octagon 8, and a decagon 10. Think of ‘octo‑pus’ for 8 legs and ‘decade’ for 10 years to never forget the side count again.

mantras.title: Science: The Mpemba Magic
mantras.text: In some strange cases, hot water actually freezes faster than cold water. This unexpected trick is called the Mpemba effect—and scientists are still arguing about why it works.

mantras.title: Maths: Roman Zero Mystery
mantras.text: The Romans had no zero symbol. They counted with I (1), V (5), X (10), L (50), and C (100)—but they had no way to write ‘nothing’ as a number.

mantras.title: Science: Moon Bounce Gravity
mantras.text: Moon gravity is about 1/6th of Earth’s. On the Moon, you could jump like a superhero—6 times higher than on Earth, without even trying.

mantras.title: Maths: The Magic of 9
mantras.text: Multiply 9 by any number from 1 to 10, then add the digits of the answer—surprise! They always add up to 9. Math never fails at this cute little pattern.

mantras.title: Science: Plants Are Air Swappers
mantras.text: Plants breathe in Carbon Dioxide and breathe out Oxygen—the opposite of humans. Thanks to plants, every breath you take is a borrowed plant exhale.

mantras.title: Maths: The Never‑Ending Pi
mantras.text: Pi (3.14159...) is the ratio of a circle’s circumference to its diameter, and its digits go on forever and do not settle into a repeating pattern. It’s an endless number that fits a perfect circle!

mantras.title: Science: Lightning Fireball
mantras.text: A single lightning bolt can be up to 5 times hotter than the surface of the Sun—over 50,000 degrees Fahrenheit—yet it lasts only fractions of a second.

mantras.title: Maths: Even vs Odd Rules
mantras.text: Even + Even = Even. Odd + Odd = Even. But Even + Odd is always Odd. These little rules make even and odd numbers behave like best friends and rivals in math.

mantras.title: Science: Human–Banana Family
mantras.text: Humans share almost 50% of their DNA with bananas. That means you’re essentially half‑banana at the molecular level!

mantras.title: Maths: Centi, Milli, Kilo Metric Hacks
mantras.text: Centi, milli, and kilo are metric word hacks. Centi means 1/100 (100 centimeters in a meter), milli means 1/1,000 (1,000 milligrams in a gram), and kilo means 1,000 (1,000 grams in a kilogram). These prefixes are tiny math cheat codes!

mantras.title: Science: Clouds That Weigh Elephants
mantras.text: Even though they look light and fluffy, a normal cloud can weigh as much as 1,000,000 pounds—about 100 adult elephants packed into the sky!

mantras.title: Maths: Triangle Angle Secret
mantras.text: No matter how crazy a triangle looks, if you add up its three inside angles, they always exactly equal 180 degrees. It’s a triangle rule that never breaks.

mantras.title: Science: Platypus Without a Stomach
mantras.text: Platypuses do not have a proper stomach—food goes from their esophagus straight to the intestine. Evolution skipped the stomach step for this weird egg‑laying mammal.

mantras.title: Maths: The Even Divisibility Trick
mantras.text: Any number that ends in 0, 2, 4, 6, or 8 is even—and can be cleanly divided by 2. Just look at the last digit and you’ll know instantly whether it’s even!

mantras.title: Science: Rust as a Three‑Ingredient Recipe
mantras.text: Iron rusts only when it touches both water and oxygen at the same time. No water or no oxygen—and the iron stays shine‑bright.

mantras.title: Maths: Hours in the Year
mantras.text: A normal year has 8,760 hours and a leap year has 8,784. That’s over 8,000 chances to learn, play, and discover something new.

mantras.title: Science: Jupiter, the Giant Swallower
mantras.text: Jupiter is so huge that all the other planets could fit inside it—twice over. It’s the heavyweight champion of our solar system.

mantras.title: Maths: Square Numbers Are Perfect Squares
mantras.text: A square number is what you get when you multiply a number by itself. 1×1=1, 2×2=4, 3×3=9—each forms a perfect grid you can draw on paper.

mantras.title: Science: Human Teeth vs Shark Teeth
mantras.text: Human teeth are as strong, pound‑for‑pound, as shark teeth. The difference? Sharks can regrow theirs over and over, while we get only one spare set.

mantras.title: Maths: The Clock’s 6‑Degree Tick
mantras.text: A clock is a circle of 360 degrees. Since there are 60 minutes, every minute pushes the minute hand forward by 6 degrees—math is ticking along all day!

mantras.title: Science: Earth’s Hidden Speed
mantras.text: Earth spins at about 1,000 miles per hour at the equator and orbits the Sun at around 67,000 miles per hour. Yet we feel nothing—an invisible cosmic roller‑coaster!
